title: George Roussos

'''George Roussos''' was a comic book artist who worked on a few issues of the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series as well as issues in the [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars: Droids'' (1986)]] series as a colorist. Roussos' earliest work in comics was assisting [[Wikipedia:Batman|Batman]] creator [[Wikipedia:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]] on the famous caped crusader's comic book in [[1940]]. He worked for nearly every comic company through the decades, and inked some of [[Wikipedia:Jack Kirby|Jack Kirby's]] early work at [[Marvel Comics]] in the 1960s under the pseudonym '''George Bell'''. Later he become a full time colorist at Marvel and would hold that position through the 1990s. He died as a result of a heart attack in 2000.
